text: Code reviews are an essential practice in software development. When team members review each other's code, they can catch bugs early, before these errors reach the final product or cause bigger issues later. This makes the whole development process more efficient and less stressful. Beyond fixing mistakes, code reviews also encourage knowledge sharing. Experienced developers can teach newer colleagues better techniques, while fresh perspectives can offer creative solutions. Over time, this collaboration improves the entire team's quality. Everyone learns from one another, coding standards become more consistent, and the whole group takes shared responsibility for the codebase. A strong review culture ultimately leads to better software.
